candidates.reason: The current image is significantly overexposed compared to the target, with blown-out highlights in the sky and on the dress. A strong reduction in exposure is the most direct way to address this primary issue.

The highlights are severely clipped, and the shadows are too bright. Pulling down highlights will recover detail in the sky and dress, while darkening shadows will add depth to the groom's suit, matching the target's tonality.
The target image is cooler and has a noticeable magenta tint compared to the current image (tint_delta: -12.84). This adjustment aims to correct the overall color cast.

The most significant color difference is the sky/water, which is washed out in the current image but a vibrant blue in the target. The HSL residuals for blues are highest. This targeted edit aims to restore the color and depth of the water.
The target image is more saturated overall (saturation_delta: 10.98). Boosting global saturation will make the colors, particularly the water and skin tones, more vibrant and closer to the target.
The current image looks flat and washed out. Increasing contrast and deepening the black point will add punch and depth, helping to define the subjects and match the richer look of the target image.
The current image is significantly brighter than the target, as shown by the large negative brightness_delta (-13.83). A global exposure reduction is the most direct way to address this.
